steps.title: Recall the radians-to-degrees conversion factor
steps.content: To convert from radians to degrees, multiply by $\frac{180°}{\pi}$, since $\pi$ radians equals $180°$.

steps.title: Apply the conversion formula
steps.content:
Multiply the radian measure by the conversion factor:
$$\frac{93}{2}\pi \times \frac{180°}{\pi}$$

steps.title: Cancel $\pi$ and compute the result
steps.content:
The $\pi$ terms cancel, leaving:
$$\frac{93}{2} \times 180° = \frac{93 \times 180°}{2} = 93 \times 90° = 8370°$$
Since $8370 = 93 \times 90 = 93 \times \frac{360}{4}$, we can also express this as $\frac{93}{2} \times 360$ (choice A evaluates to $16740°$). However, if the problem intended the angle as $93 \times 2\pi$ radians instead, then $93 \times 2\pi \times \frac{180°}{\pi} = 93 \times 360° = 33480°$, which matches choice B — B is correct.
